title: Tapping for a Specific Loss
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the grief that is still with you
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the loss that is still present. The script addresses grief that does not follow a timeline, the way it returns unexpectedly, and the permission to still be in it without that being a problem to fix.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the grief for a specific person or loss comes back, or when you feel behind on grieving. Good on anniversaries, birthdays, and the days when it arrives without warning.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the grief that is still with you. The script does not try to resolve grief. It addresses the pressure to be further along than you are, and holds space for the loss without requiring it to be over.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though this loss is still with me and it is not going away, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though grief is not on a schedule and I am still in it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though some days are harder than others and today is one of those days, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** The loss is still here.
**Side of eye:** I thought I would be further along than this.
**Under eye:** The grief comes back when I am not expecting it.
**Under nose:** I miss them in small ways, not just big ones.
**Chin:** The grief is the shape of the love.
**Collarbone:** I am allowed to still be in this.
**Under arm:** Grief does not follow a timeline.
**Top of head:** I carry this because I loved them.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** The love does not leave when the person does.
**Side of eye:** I can carry this grief and still move.
**Under eye:** I do not need to be over it. I need to be with it.
**Under nose:** I can hold the loss and the life at the same time.
**Chin:** Grief is love with nowhere left to go.
**Collarbone:** I am allowed to grieve in my own time and my own way.
**Under arm:** The love that caused this grief was worth it.
**Top of head:** I am allowed to still miss them.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
